introduction: Views provide a virtual table based on the result-set of a query, offering a layer of security and abstraction. Indexes are physical structures on disk that speed up data retrieval.

sections.title: Views in SQL
sections.content: A view contains rows and columns, just like a real table. The fields in a view are fields from one or more real tables in the database. You can add SQL functions, WHERE, and JOIN statements to a view.
sections.code:
CREATE VIEW [Brazil Customers] AS
SELECT CustomerName, ContactName FROM Customers WHERE Country = 'Brazil';

sections.title: Indexes
sections.content: Indexes are used to retrieve data from the database more quickly than otherwise. The users cannot see the indexes, they are just used to speed up searches/queries. Updating a table with indexes takes more time than updating a table without.

realWorldApplication.title: Netflix Search & Kids Profiles
realWorldApplication.scenario: When you search Netflix for 'Action', the database doesn't check all 100,000 movies one-by-one; it uses an **Index** to jump instantly to the answer. Furthermore, when a child logs into a Kids Profile, Netflix uses a **View**—a virtual table that filters out R-rated movies, providing a safe, restricted lens into the main database without duplicating data.
